title: penstemon purpusii
intro: Penstemon purpusii, also known as the Mexican penstemon, is a species of flowering plant in the penstemon family, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Penstemon purpusii include its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and reduce fever. The plant can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and its leaves and flowers can be used to make a topical salve for skin conditions such as wounds and burns.
